fullText: hello guys in this video i will test the new model miniax m2.7 because i received this email probably automated maybe even written by ai agent because ronson i don't even know without surname i have a suspicion it may be automatic but anyway they are inviting to test and why not because i see people mentioning miniax new model on social media on twitter and let's see if the coding abilities of that model has improved since m2.5 which i tested roughly a month ago here's my table of evaluation at the time for laravel project and miniax m2.5 at the time for two evaluation rounds was actually the worst performing model out of those in the list so in this video i will try to launch four projects four prompts on the same projects that m2.5 failed and let's see if it's improved so my methodology of evaluation is this a project on laravel it's laravel 12 now laravel 13 is out but it doesn't really matter that much for this testing basically there's a task in this prompt so there's isolation bug for multi-tenency and i will give this prompt to the model and then i have set of evaluation tests so this is the set of tests that i will run but those tests are not in the same project they are copied from elsewhere so the agent wouldn't even know the evaluation criteria all it knows is this prompt and it has to fix the bug and i will try minax with the help of vs code and client extension and in client i can use open router and choose the model like minimax m2.5 or the new m2.7 so let's launch that prompt i paste here and let's see what happens it is thinking it's starting to work and also we will see the price so i added $5 to my open router account for this test but probably it will be less than $5 for total four tests we'll see okay so it started to work it started to generate some code and we see 12 items on the to-do list so i will come back to this video when it finished the first project okay and roughly 7 minutes in i see it's running in circles trying to fix the tests related to livewire so it's probably not familiar with livewire and flux ui and how layouts work with livewire but it's still trying so i will wait for it to finish and we're also 24 cents in on the price and it is finally completed in roughly 10 minutes with 48 passed tests but the test of itself so it's written the test itself and verified so the task was completed but now my evaluation tests will they work so here's my command copy the test from outside and then run php artisan test and let's see if it succeeds and six out of 10 tests fail so mismatch between 404 and 403 expected response status code is basically not the one that i was expecting and actually some of that could be debatable because on permission issues sometimes it does make sense to return 404 just for security to not even show 403 but looking at the code this is the code of controller and miniax is using gate denies and throwing authorization exception manually instead of using laravel features like policy or other native laravel way to do the same thing so this is totally a red flag and unacceptable so the first out of four projects test fail the second project in the same fashion this is the prompt project with starter kit and the task is to add a package so add a package for laravel permission and then implement the permissions for roles and how minimax would deal with that so we put in the prompt and by the way the previous prompt cost 39 and let's see what happens this time and by the way i will shorten this test to three projects out of four because fourth is pretty complex to set up let's see what miniax will be capable of from three tests and it's done faster this time in roughly 5 minutes so it didn't run in circles with automated testing here's the summary the price is 12 cents the tests are passing and now the evaluation time so again terminal command to copy the tests and run php artisan test and 29 passed it was successful so the task about permission that miniax m2.5 failed m2.7 was successful and now the third test which actually a lot of models failed less known package and this is the prompt for the third project so the task is to install a less known package called spotty laravel model states which is not like top 10 in laravel community but the instruction is pretty clear install the package and then use the states like this so again i open client i also had one failed test you can see behind the scenes that doesn't count because i didn't prepare the project properly now it is done and we can paste the prompt and start rolling again and there we go 7 minutes in we have first failed test so 14 failed tests with the error that some state class not found so maybe something with name spaces it may be a quick fix but we'll see in general i see the problem with any llms actually when they generate the code they run the test and only then they realize that something is wrong and then sometimes for some models it's running in circles trying to fix the test which may lead to refactoring of maybe the main part of the code and after some fixes now we have 11 failed tests the last time i saw and the message is different so it's kind of a progress and finally after a few more rounds in circles after 15 minutes in total it finally succeeded with tests which cost us 59 cents and almost on the edge of hallucinating because running out of context of 200k tokens and let's see the evaluation tests again tests are copied from elsewhere and we're running passing passing failed some failed so some statuses are incorrect pending and delivered another failure is pending and shipped so something is incorrect with the states not according to the official specification also i see the error of undefined property in some view so yeah this test unfortunately is failed as well out of curiosity let's take a look at the code how good is it so these are the files edited let's take a look at for example model of order so have the states from the package seems okay a few more helpful methods private methods okay what about controller what new do we have the method transition it uses form request validated try catch looks okay to me with catching different exceptions looks okay we have one new route we have something in blade well that thing i personally don't like the inline php in blade according to mvc classics i would prefer that to be in controller but it's kind of a personal preference now in latest laravel versions people are more loose with that classical mvc and factory with states also looks good so the code itself the structure is okay but it didn't deliver on the actual prompt based on the evaluation so something was wrong with the logic how it performed the prompt and the task so what can i say to ronson in reply with that email the good news is out of three failing tests one is not failing anymore so there is progress from m2.5 but looking at how it evaluates the code and the process there's still a lot of running in circles about failing tests so this is probably the symptom of deeper issues with longer running tasks for like 5 to 10 minutes which i usually evaluate the models on so still i would consider miniax quite far from the frontier models like opus and codex and actually at that time one month ago codex was the only model that performed flawlessly opos and sonnet failed on a few tasks as well this can be discarded because legacy project was incorrectly prepared with kind of a vague prompt but yeah with miniax it's cheaper so for example for those three tasks on open router i spent 57 cents in total so what is shown in vs code in client is not actually accurate the actual numbers on open router are lower than vs code and client shows and if you compare the prices without any subscription on miniax m27 it's 30 cents per input and 120 per output compared to for example kimik 2.5 which is 45 and 220 more expensive and i'm not even talking about something like opus which is like $5 and $25 respectively and what about gpt for example yeah if you use it with open router it's also very expensive but of course for entropic and for gpt people usually use subscriptions to cloud code or codeex so yeah comparing the prices miniax is much less expensive but again you get what you're paying for so mini max may be good for smaller tasks i guess and for some agentic work but not really fully reliable yet at the moment at least for my evaluations on laravel projects what do you guys think have you tried miniax m2.7 or m2.5 earlier does it work for you and in what cases do you use that specific model let's discuss as usual in the comments below that's it for this time and see you guys in other
